Title: Discovery and Characterization of Fluopipamine, a Putative Cellulose Synthase 1 Antagonist within Arabidopsis
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase family; glycosyltransferase family 2; multi-pass transmembrane cellulose synthase catalytic subunit
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary cell wall cellulose synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that are assembled into cellulose microfibrils
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key catalytic component of the cellulose synthase complex; not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting enzyme in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutations in CESA1 confer resistance to fluopipamine, supporting CESA1 as the primary target whose inhibition reduces cellulose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural polymer of the plant cell wall required for anisotropic cell expansion and normal seedling growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; Ler used for mapping crosses
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fluopipamine treatment; comparison with isoxaben and oryzalin; dark-grown etiolated seedlings in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CESA protomers within the cellulose synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Radiolabeled [14C]glucose incorporation assay; EMS mutagenesis and resistant mutant mapping; CAPS genotyping; cross-resistance testing with known cesa1 mutants; in silico structural modeling and docking
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fluopipamine treatment reduced incorporation of radiolabeled glucose into cellulose by about 70%, consistent with inhibition of cellulose synthesis. 2. Two resistance-conferring loci/alleles mapped to CESA1, including known CESA1R292C cross-resistance and newly identified CESA1G1009S in the FPR-M line. 3. Structural modeling suggested fluopipamine may bind near the active channel and/or at the interface between CESA protomers, explaining resistance-associated mutations.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper focuses on chemical antagonism and resistance alleles rather than native metabolic regulation. Reported resistance-associated alleles include CESA1R292C and CESA1G1009S for fluopipamine; CESA1G620E showed only limited cross-resistance; other mutations discussed partly from prior studies.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Resistance-associated CESA1 alleles inform herbicide target-site resistance prediction and may guide design of improved cellulose biosynthesis inhibitors rather than nutrient-enhancement breeding.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inhibition of CESA1 causes severe growth defects including short/swollen roots, loss of anisotropic expansion, inhibited etiolation, and ectopic lignification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Next-generation mapping / bulk segregant-style SNP mapping; accession NA
